mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of months following a rate cut, or rolling Rank IC falls below 0.02 over 3 years.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in >60% of instances following a >0.5pp unemployment jump, or out-of-sample accuracy drops below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and business demand expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields negative 20-day returns in >50% of occurrences, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices during a post-recession expansion phase reflect strengthening manufacturing output and corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in >55% of months where INDPRO rises during recovery phases, or the signal predictive power vanishes outside the regime.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP reflects broad economic expansion and higher corporate profit expectations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days following data release.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following positive GDP prints are negative in >60% of cases, or the signal shows zero predictive accuracy in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation reading exceeding 0.4% triggers expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 60-day returns in >55% of months following inflation spikes above 0.4%, or the threshold effect disappears in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening and wage growth, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal predicts negative 20-day returns in >50% of observations, or the interaction effect fails to improve model fit over single-series baselines.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle signals supply-side constraints and margin compression, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >60% of months where INDPRO falls during tightening cycles, or the regime-specific predictive accuracy drops below 50%.
